{"global":{"name":"Chen Huang"},"nav":{"home":"home","about":"about","research":"research","patents":"patents","notes":"notes","gallery":"gallery","back-to-top":"back to top","random-post":"Random post","random-note":"Random note","tags":"Tags","institution":"Institution"},"home":{"greeting":"Hello, I'm","title":"Chen Huang","subtitle":"Exploring the Quantum World","resume":"Download Resume"},"about":{"title":"About Me","bio":"

Hi, I'm Chen, an incoming PhD student in Computer Science and Engineering at The Chinese University of Hong Kong (CUHK).

I am passionate about advancing quantum computing through both theoretical research and experimental realization. My academic journey has equipped me with a strong foundation in physics and hands-on experience with a variety of quantum systems, including neutral atoms, trapped ions, and semiconductor qubits.

I also work with quantum circuits. At BAQIS, my research focuses on developing efficient quantum compilation strategies that help bridge the gap between hardware capabilities and practical, scalable quantum computing.

","heroCard":{"default":"I am passionate about expanding my knowledge through interdisciplinary research and collaboration. If you are interested in discussing research or potential collaborations, feel free to reach out via email or connect with me on LinkedIn."},"nameMeaning":{"chen":"My given name is Chen, which means \"dawn\" or \"early morning\" in Chinese. This meaning suits me well, as I am a morning person who enjoys the calm and peacefulness of the early hours. Interestingly, \"Chen\" is sometimes misheard as \"Cheng\", which means \"orange\" in Chinese. Because of this playful similarity, my friends affectionately call me \"Cheng-Zi\".","huang":"My surname is Huang, which means \"yellow\" in Chinese. This name has deep historical and cultural roots. It is often associated with the Yellow Emperor, a legendary figure considered one of the founding ancestors of Chinese civilization."},"research":{"imperial":"I was an MSc student in the Controlled Quantum Dynamics Group at Imperial College London. My research focused on charge noise in semiconductor qubits. By conducting experiments and simulations, I aimed to understand the impact of charge noise on qubit performance and develop strategies to mitigate its effects.","baqis":"Simultaneously, I am also a remote research intern in the Quantum Operation System Group at the Beijing Academy of Quantum Information Sciences (BAQIS). My research focuses on quantum compilation with neutral atoms."},"news":["2026-05-21 Our paper was accepted by IEEE Transactions on Quantum Engineering (TQE)!","2026-02-24 Our paper on neutral atomic quantum circuit compilation was accepted by DAC 2026!","2026-02-02 I started my research assistantship at CUHK CSE.","2026-01-26 I received the official PhD offer from CUHK CSE. Next stop, Hong Kong!","2025-10-01 I received the Master's degree in Physics from Imperial College London with distinction.","2025-09-12 My fifth patent was filed.","2025-06-18 I completed my Master's thesis defense and received a score of 85.15/100 for my research project."]},"research":{"title":"Research","read-more":"Read More","abstract":"Abstract","collaborators":"Collaborators","links":"Links & Resources"},"patents":{"title":"Patents","see-patent":"See Patent","inventors":"Inventors"},"footer":{"copyright":"© {{year}} Chen Huang. markdownRemark.excerpt: Advanced Quantum Mechanics with Professor Yong Hu was a fantastic and challenging course. I took it as an undergraduate, and it offers deep…
markdownRemark.html:
Advanced Quantum Mechanics with Professor Yong Hu was a fantastic and challenging course. I took it as an undergraduate, and it offers deep insights for anyone with a good grasp of introductory quantum mechanics. These are my study notes from the class. (Note: written in Chinese).

Download the full notes (PDF)

One of the most interesting parts was a homework assignment on the "Quantum Swing," which I had a lot of fun simulating with a QuTiP script. You can find a write-up and the code for that project on my Zhihu page.

Check out the "Quantum Swing" project on Zhihu

The course primarily referenced J. J. Sakurai's classic textbook, Modern Quantum Mechanics.

This is just one part of my HUST course notes collection. If you find this resource helpful, I'd appreciate a star on the GitHub repository!

View on GitHub
